creator: jhale@FreeBSD.org
text:
Since Strawberry supports many formats via various GStreamer plugins that everyone may not need, the plugin installation portion has been intentionally left up to the user. There is a pkg-message `pkg info -D strawberry-qt*` that informs the user that they need to install plugins for whatever audio format they want to use. We did the same thing with recently removed port multimedia/phonon-gstreamer. It was meant to reduce bloat and allow for a more customizable installation, but it's becoming more apparent to me that this approach is too confusing and plugins for more popular formats should probably just be hard dependencies.
 
I'm pretty sure the chromaprint, neon, shout2, soup, and twolame plugins are not used by Strawberry in any way. The cdparanoia plugin should be added to the CDIO option though, since it is required to read CDs and it may not be obvious to the user to install it.
